name: Pharmaceutical Drug Residue Removal Dataset
description: Comprehensive contamination dataset for Pharmaceutical Drug Residue. Includes chemical composition, safety metrics, PPE requirements, and laser removal parameters for effective contamination removal.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser contamination removal analysis, safety assessment, chemical characterization

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive database of contamination removal parameters, safety data, and laser cleaning specifications

contaminant.name: Pharmaceutical Drug Residue
contaminant.description: Pharmaceutical-residue contamination, it arises from active ingredient deposits during equipment processing. This residue, it forms sticky layers and differs from mineral scales by organic stickiness that bonds deeply. On stainless steel and glass, it clings unevenly and defies solvents, so removal challenges persist; laser cleaning, it vaporizes residue selectively and leaves surfaces intact.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Pyrolytic Carbon Particles, Carbon Monoxide, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
